name: Grilled Swordfish with Orange, Onion and Toma
recipeIngredient: 2 medium oranges, peeled, seeded and cut into 1/4-inch pieces
1 large tomato, cored, seeded and cut into 1/4-inch dice
2 lbs swordfish, about 3/4-inch thick, cut into 4 equal pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine orange juice, vinegar and brown sugar. Cook the mixture, uncovered, over low boil until reduced to about 1/2 cup, stirring often to prevent scorching.
2. Meanwhile, in a colander or strainer, combine oranges, tomato and onion. Drain well and transfer to a bowl. Add cilantro and gently stir in all but 2 tablespoons of the hot orange juice syrup.
3. When ready to grill, rinse fish and pat dry. Brush both sides of the fish with the remaining orange juice mixture.
4. Place fish on oiled grill over hot coals and cook, turning once, until fish is opaque in the center, about 6-7 minutes total.
5. Remove from grill, transfer to serving platter and spoon relish over top.
